title: The dark as a soft blanket
subtitle: A short visualisation for replacing the fear-association with darkness with a safety-association.
excerpt: A brief visualisation from Day 12 of the SLEEP program. The practice replaces the body's fear-image of darkness with a simple, domestic one: darkness as weight, warmth, and cover.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education as a companion visualisation for Day 12 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use in the early evening, before the anticipatory dread has fully built. The aim is to interrupt the fear-prediction and give the body a different image of what darkness means.

content.text: A brief visualisation from Day 12 of the SLEEP program. The image is simple and sensory: darkness as something that settles on you like a blanket, not something that comes for you.
Sit or lie comfortably. Close your eyes. Three slow breaths.
Picture the room going dark — not suddenly, the way a switch is flipped, but gradually, the way evening comes. The light through the curtains softening. The edges of the furniture going quiet.

Now picture the darkness as something with weight and warmth. Not empty space — something present. A soft blanket settling slowly over the room, over you, over everything that needed to be done today.
The blanket is heavy enough to feel real, light enough to breathe under. It doesn't require anything from you. It just settles.
Let the image sit for a minute. Let your shoulders drop into it. Open your eyes when ready.
Original to homemade.education as a companion visualisation for Day 12 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
